records.ety: [OF. espouser, esposer, F. pouser, L. sponsare to betroth, espouse, fr. sponsus betrothed, p. p. of spondere to promise solemnly or sacredly. Cf. Spouse.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Espoused (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Espousing.]

defs: To betroth; to promise in marriage; to give as spouse.
quotes: A virgin espoused to a man whose name was Joseph.

defs: To take as spouse; to take to wife; to marry.
quotes: Lavinia will I make my empress, . . . And in the sacred Pantheon her espouse.

defs: To take to one's self with a view to maintain; to make one's own; to take up the cause of; to adopt; to embrace.
quotes: Promised faithfully to espouse his cause as soon as he got out of the war.
